title.zh: 真菌「壓力盾牌」新解：兩種酵素合成特殊聚合物，讓病菌突破植物防線
title.en: How Fungi Build Pressure-Proof Shields: A New Mechanism for Plant Invasion

summary.zh: 【總結】這項研究揭開植物病原真菌入侵植物的關鍵機制：兩種酵素「PKS2」和「PBG13」合成3,5-二羥基己酸（DHHA）聚合物，形成細胞壁的半透膜屏障。當病原真菌形成吸器（appressoria）時，這些聚合物使細胞壁具有選擇性通透性，允許水分進入卻阻擋溶質滲漏，從而建立高滲透壓（約3.7 MPa），像微型水壓機一樣頂破植物表皮。這不僅挑戰了過去認為「黑色素是唯一關鍵因素」的觀點，更為農業病害防治提供了全新靶點，可能幫助應對全球糧食安全威脅。
summary.en: **Summary:** This study uncovers a groundbreaking mechanism by which plant-pathogenic fungi generate turgor pressure to breach host surfaces: the enzymes PKS2 and PBG13 synthesize 3,5-dihydroxyhexanoic acid (DHHA) polymers that form a semipermeable barrier in appressoria cell walls. These polymers allow water influx while blocking osmolyte leakage, creating a pressure of ~3.7 MPa—enough to rupture plant epidermis like a microscopic hydraulic jack. Challenging the long-held view that melanin alone enables turgor generation, this discovery opens new avenues for controlling devastating crop diseases that threaten global food security.

keyArticle.title: Dihydroxyhexanoic acid biosynthesis controls turgor in pathogenic fungi
